      <title>Hundreds feared dead in attempt to cross Mediterranean during cyclone</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/26/hundreds-feared-dead-after-attempting-to-cross-mediterranean-amid-cyclone-harry</link>
      <description><p>Fifty killed in one incident as Italian authorities estimate 380 people may have drowned last week</p><p>Up to 380 people may have drowned attempting to cross the Mediterranean last week as Cyclone Harry battered southern Italy and Malta, the Italian coastguard has said, as a shipwreck with the loss of 50 lives was confirmed by Maltese authorities.</p><p>Just one person, who was hospitalised in Malta, survived the shipwreck, which happened on Friday.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/26/hundreds-feared-dead-after-attempting-to-cross-mediterranean-amid-cyclone-harry">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Crocodile warnings as floods devastate southern Africa</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/26/crocodile-warnings-floods-devastate-southern-africa</link>
      <description><p>More than 100 people killed and hundreds of thousands displaced in South Africa, Mozambique and Zimbabwe</p><p>Devastating floods have killed more than 100 people in southern Africa since the beginning of the year and displaced hundreds of thousands, as authorities and aid workers warn of hunger, cholera and attacks by crocodiles that have spread with the waters.</p><p>More than 70 people have died in Zimbabwe and 30 in South Africa, where hundreds of people were evacuated from Kruger national park earlier this month after a <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2026/jan/16/weather-tracer-extreme-rainfall-inundates-south-africa-mozambique-flood-warning">deluge of rain</a>.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/26/crocodile-warnings-floods-devastate-southern-africa">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>‘It’s the sovereignty of the country’: Guinea-Bissau says US vaccine study suspended</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/23/guinea-bissau-hepatitis-b-vaccine-study</link>
      <description><p>Despite US pushback, officials in west Africa say controversial hepatitis B study on pause amid ethics concerns</p><p>US health officials insisted it was still on. African health leaders said it was <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/15/hepatitis-b-vaccines-study-africa-cancel">cancelled</a>. At the heart of the controversy is the west African nation of Guinea-Bissau – one of the poorest countries in the world and the proposed site of a hotly debated US-funded study on vaccines.</p><p>The study on hepatitis B vaccination, to be led by Danish researchers, became a flashpoint after major changes to the US vaccination schedule and prompted questions about how research is conducted ethically in other countries.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/23/guinea-bissau-hepatitis-b-vaccine-study">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>ActionAid to rethink child sponsorship as part of plan to ‘decolonise’ its work</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/22/actionaid-rethink-child-sponsorship-decolonise-funding</link>
      <description><p>Development charity’s new co-chief executives signal shift from controversial sponsor a child scheme launched in 1972 to long-term grassroots funding</p><p>Child sponsorship schemes that allow donors to handpick children to support in poor countries can carry racialised, paternalistic undertones and need to be transformed, the newly appointed co-chief executives of ActionAid UK said as they set out to “decolonise” the organisation’s work.</p><p>ActionAid began in 1972 by finding sponsors for schoolchildren in India and Kenya, but Taahra Ghazi and Hannah Bond have launched their co-leadership this month with the goal of shifting narratives around aid from sympathy towards solidarity and partnership with global movements.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/22/actionaid-rethink-child-sponsorship-decolonise-funding">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Head of US Africa bureau urges staff to highlight US ‘generosity’ despite aid cuts</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/20/us-diplomats-urged-to-remind-african-leaders-of-us-generosity-despite-usaid-closing</link>
      <description><p>Email sent to diplomats by state department office’s new boss is labelled ‘racist’ after dismissing Africa as a priority</p><p>US diplomats have been encouraged to “unabashedly and aggressively” remind African governments about the “generosity” of the American people, according to a leaked email sent to staff in the US state department’s Bureau of African Affairs this January and obtained by the Guardian.</p><p>“It’s not gauche to remind these countries of the American people’s generosity in containing HIV/Aids or alleviating famine,” says the email.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/20/us-diplomats-urged-to-remind-african-leaders-of-us-generosity-despite-usaid-closing">Continue reading...</a></description>

    <item>
      <title>Can Venezuela’s Delcy Rodríguez become a Latin American Deng Xiaoping?</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/28/can-venezuelas-delcy-rodriguez-become-a-latin-american-deng-xiaoping</link>
      <description><p>Maduro’s Sorbonne-educated successor is talking up an era of ‘reform and opening up’ modelled on China’s post-Mao boom</p><p>After years of political and social upheaval, hunger and despair, the Great Helmsman departs and is replaced by a francophile economic reformer who catapults a traumatised country into a new era of prosperity and growth<em>.</em></p><p>That is what happened in China half a century ago when <a href="https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/commandingheights/shared/minitext/prof_dengxiaoping.html">the croissant-loving communist</a> Deng Xiaoping became paramount leader after Chairman Mao Zedong’s 1976 death and set in motion one of history’s biggest economic booms.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/28/can-venezuelas-delcy-rodriguez-become-a-latin-american-deng-xiaoping">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <category domain="https://www.theguardian.com/world/xi-jinping">Xi Jinping</category>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/27/mexico-cuba-trump-oil</link>
      <description><p>Claudia Sheinbaum denied move was response to pressure from the US, after Trump said ‘zero’ oil would go to Cuba</p><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/mexico">Mexico</a> has cancelled a shipment of oil to <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/cuba">Cuba</a>, the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, appeared to confirm on Tuesday, but she insisted the decision was “sovereign” and not a response to pressure from the US.</p><p>Fuel shortages are causing increasingly severe blackouts in Cuba, and Mexico has been the island’s biggest oil supplier since the US blocked shipments from Venezuela last month.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/27/mexico-cuba-trump-oil">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Starmer-Xi meeting live: UK prime minister says he wants ‘more sophisticated’ relationship with China</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2026/jan/29/starmer-xi-jinping-meeting-uk-china-relationship-live</link>
      <description><p>Starmer tells Xi Jinping during Beijing meeting that it has been ‘too long’ since a British PM has visited China.</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/jan/29/xi-starmer-china-uk-meeting-chinese-leader-rise-above-differences-sophisticated">Full report: Xi says he hopes China and UK can ‘rise above differences’</a></p></li></ul><p>For more context on today’s <strong>Starmer</strong>-<strong>Xi</strong> meeting, China is the world’s second-biggest economy and Britain’s third-largest trading partner – to which it exports £45bn of goods and services a year – so it is no surprise the UK has turned to Beijing in its search for economic reliability.</p><p>As the Guardian’s political editor <strong><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/jan/29/keir-starmer-xi-jinping-china-economy-politics">Pippa Crerar</a></strong><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/jan/29/keir-starmer-xi-jinping-china-economy-politics"> reported</a> earlier today, the UK does not rank among the top 10 of China’s trading partners but the Beijing leadership has spied a political opportunity to improve links with one of Washington’s closest allies at a time of deep uncertainty in the transatlantic alliance.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/live/2026/jan/29/starmer-xi-jinping-meeting-uk-china-relationship-live">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/29/thursday-briefing-will-keir-starmers-cautious-china-gamble-pay-off</link>
      <description><p>In today’s newsletter: With China now central to the world’s green tech​ and economic future, the UK faces a ​s​eries of strategic ​discussions it can no longer postpone</p><p></p><p>Good morning. The Starmer has landed.</p><p>Yesterday, Keir Starmer became the first British prime minister to make the trip to China since Theresa May’s in 2018 (meaning a surprisingly large number of PMs didn’t) and has vowed to bring “stability and clarity” to <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/27/keir-starmer-walks-tightrope-over-myriad-issues-in-quest-to-bolster-china-ties">the UK’s approach to Beijing</a>.</p><p><em><strong>Iran </strong></em>| Donald Trump has warned time is running out for Tehran and said <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/28/trump-threat-us-iran-war-armada-nuclear-programme">a massive US armada was moving quickly</a> towards the country.</p><p><em><strong>Assisted Dying </strong></em>| Supporters of assisted dying will seek to force <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/jan/28/assisted-dying-supporters-to-force-through-bill-with-archaic-commons-procedure">through the bill</a> using an archaic parliamentary procedure if it continues to be blocked by the Lords.</p><p><em><strong>UK politics </strong></em>| Centrist ideas are <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/politics/2026/jan/28/centrist-ideas-no-longer-wanted-in-conservative-party-says-kemi-badenoch">no longer wanted</a> in the Conservative party, Kemi Badenoch has said.</p><p><em><strong>Ofsted </strong></em>| A <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/education/2026/jan/28/ofsted-brunel-academy-bristol-report-damien-egan-visit">snap inspection</a> of a Bristol secondary school criticised for postponing a visit by an MP who is a member of a group that advocates for Israel has found “no evidence of partisan political views”.</p><p><em><strong>BBC </strong></em>| The BBC has named senior executive Rhodri Talfan Davies as its <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/media/2026/jan/28/bbc-names-rhodri-talfan-davies-as-interim-general-director">interim director general</a>, as the corporation continues the search for a permanent replacement for Tim Davie.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/29/thursday-briefing-will-keir-starmers-cautious-china-gamble-pay-off">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Lawyer for Guan Heng, whose exposed evidence of persecution of Uyghurs, says he is ‘textbook example of why asylum should exist’</p><p>A US immigration judge has granted asylum to a Chinese national who he said had a “well founded fear” of persecution if sent back to China after exposing alleged human rights abuses against Uyghurs there.</p><p>Guan Heng applied for asylum after arriving in the US illegally in 2021.       <title>South Korea’s ‘world-first’ AI laws face pushback amid bid to become leading tech power</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/29/south-korea-world-first-ai-regulation-laws</link>
      <description><p>The laws have been criticised by tech startups, which say they go too far, and civil society groups, which say they don’t go far enough</p><p>South Korea has embarked on a foray into the regulation of AI, launching what has been billed as the most comprehensive set of laws anywhere in the world, that could prove a model for other countries, but the new legislation has already encountered pushback.</p><p>The laws, which will force companies to label AI-generated content, have been criticised by local tech startups, which say they go too far, and civil society groups, which say they don’t go far enough.</p><p>Add invisible digital watermarks for clearly artificial outputs such as cartoons or artwork. For realistic deepfakes, visible labels are required.</p><p>“High-impact AI”, including systems used for <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/ng-interactive/2026/jan/24/how-the-confident-authority-of-google-ai-overviews-is-putting-public-health-at-risk">medical diagnosis</a>, hiring and loan approvals, will require operators to conduct risk assessments and document how decisions are made. If a human makes the final decision the system may fall outside the category.</p><p>Extremely powerful AI models will require safety reports, but the threshold is set so high that government officials acknowledge no models worldwide currently meet it.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/29/south-korea-world-first-ai-regulation-laws">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Albanese defends Isaac Herzog’s Australia visit as federal MP joins calls to rescind invitation</title>

      <description><p>Independent MP Sophie Scamps joins Labor’s Friends of Palestine group and Greens senator Mehreen Faruqi in objecting to Israeli president’s visit</p><ul><li><p><a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/live/2026/jan/29/victoria-bushfires-weather-nsw-protest-laws-coalition-split-sussan-ley-david-littleproud-ntwnfb">Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates</a></p></li><li><p>Get our <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/email-newsletters?CMP=cvau_sfl">breaking news email</a>, <a href="https://app.adjust.com/w4u7jx3">free app</a> or <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/series/full-story?CMP=cvau_sfl">daily news podcast</a></p></li></ul><p>Anthony Albanese has defended the government’s invitation to Israeli president, Isaac Herzog, to visit, after another federal MP joined calls to cancel the visit after the Bondi terror attack.</p><p>Herzog’s arrival in February will be met by a “major security response” in Sydney and planned protests nationwide from pro-Palestinian groups demonstrating against the civilian death toll from Israel’s war on Gaza.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/australia-news/2026/jan/29/albanese-defends-isaac-herzogs-australia-visit-as-federal-mp-joins-calls-to-rescind-invitation-ntwnfb">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Calls for independent external inquiry into brutal crackdown that some estimates suggest killed more than 30,000</p><p>A deep and painful inquest is under way inside Iran as politicians, academics and the security establishment try to come to terms with what has been described as a catastrophe after the violent protests and their <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/27/iran-protests-death-toll-disappeared-bodies-mass-burials-30000-dead">even more violent suppression</a> by the security forces.</p><p>The shape of the debate taking place in the heavily censored society is emerging, as selective newspapers and Telegram channels slowly open up to international audiences after the protests – which some estimates suggest could have left more than <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/27/iran-protests-death-toll-disappeared-bodies-mass-burials-30000-dead">30,000 dead</a> – that have stunned many Iranians.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/29/iran-confront-catastrophe-violent-crackdown-protests">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/29/iran-doctors-arrested-treating-injured-protesters</link>
      <description><p>US state department calls for the release of all detained healthcare workers as at least one arrested surgeon reported to be at risk of execution</p><p>Doctors are being arrested in Iran for helping save the lives of some of the tens of thousands injured during <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/10/iran-protesters-tell-of-brutal-police-response-deaths-and-forced-confessions">Iran’s brutal crackdown on anti-regime protests</a>, with at least one surgeon now at risk of being sentenced to death.</p><p>The arrests and death sentence are part of a campaign of “revenge”, say human rights groups, after healthcare workers and doctors refused to ignore the plight of badly injured protesters shot or stabbed at close range, and in some cases set up makeshift treatment centres.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/29/iran-doctors-arrested-treating-injured-protesters">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <pubDate>Thu, 29 Jan 2026 05:00:36 GMT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2026/jan/29/iran-doctors-arrested-treating-injured-protesters</guid>

      <description><p>Experts say uneven connectivity suggests regime is throttling and filtering data as losses said to hit $36m a day</p><p>Iranian authorities appear to have relaxed – but not removed – internet restrictions, in what experts say is a sign of the mounting costs of the most severe internet blackout the regime has ever imposed.</p><p>“There seems to be a real patchwork of connectivity.       <link>https://www.theguardian.com/education/2026/jan/29/school-suspension-in-england-only-to-be-for-pupils-most-serious-misbehaviour</link>
      <description><p>Policy intended to keep more children sanctioned for non-violent bad behaviour in school in ‘internal exclusion’ units</p><p>Suspending pupils from school will be reserved for the most serious cases of bad behaviour including violence, according to the latest government guidance to be issued to schools in England.</p><p>The Department for Education (DfE) is to announce a consultation on behaviour policy to be included in the forthcoming schools white paper that is intended to keep more children sanctioned for non-violent misbehaviour in schools in units known as “internal exclusion”, rather than sending them home.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/education/2026/jan/29/school-suspension-in-england-only-to-be-for-pupils-most-serious-misbehaviour">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Average bills to go up again in April, with Ofwat approving record spending plans amid outrage over sewage spills</p><p>Water bills in England and Wales will rise by an average of £33 per household in April, in the latest above-inflation increase intended to fix leaking pipes and sewage treatment works.</p><p>The increase will push the average annual water bill to £639 in the year from 1 April, up 5.4% on the previous year, according to figures published on Thursday by <a href="https://www.water.org.uk/">Water UK</a>, a lobby group for the industry.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/money/2026/jan/29/households-in-england-and-wales-must-splash-out-more-as-water-bills-rise-again">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <description><p>Of those affected by midlife challenges such as menopause, nine in 10 do not seek help, therapists’ organisation says</p><p>Almost two in three women over 50 in the UK struggle with their mental health as they deal with menopause, relationship breakdowns and changes to their appearance, a survey has found.</p><p>Brain fog, parents dying, children leaving home and financial pressures can also trigger difficulties such as sleeping problems, feeling anxious or overwhelmed, and a loss of zest for life.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/society/2026/jan/29/women-uk-50-plus-midlife-menopause-struggle-mental-health">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>FBI executes search warrant at election office in Fulton county, Georgia</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/28/fbi-search-warrant-fulton-county-georgia</link>
      <description><p>County spokesperson says warrant seeks number of 2020 election records while FBI declines to give further details</p><p>The FBI executed a search warrant at the election office in Fulton county, Georgia, on Wednesday for records related to the 2020 election, according to a spokesperson for the county and the FBI.</p><p>The warrant sought all ballots from the 2020 election in Fulton county, tabulator tapes, ballot images and voter rolls, according to a warrant obtained by the Guardian.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/28/fbi-search-warrant-fulton-county-georgia">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Senate Democrats demand DHS funding bill include reforms to ‘rein in ICE’</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/28/democrats-dhs-funding-bill-reform-ice</link>
      <description><p>Move sets up clash with Republicans that could see government partially shut down in coming days</p><p>Chuck Schumer, the Senate’s top Democrat, on Wednesday demanded that legislation funding the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) include a ban on wearing masks and other reforms aimed at federal agents involved in immigration enforcement, setting up a clash with Republicans that could see the government partially shut down in the coming days.</p><p>Congress is racing to head off a lapse in federal funding from beginning after Friday, and the Senate’s Republican leaders plan to hold a key procedural vote on Thursday on the DHS funding measure and five bills that authorize spending by several other government departments.</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jan/28/democrats-dhs-funding-bill-reform-ice">Continue reading...</a></description>

      <title>Tesla discontinues Model X and S vehicles as Elon Musk pivots to robotics</title>
      <link>https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jan/28/tesla-q4-earnings-estimates-elon-musk</link>
      <description><p>High hopes for Optimus robot help company beat forecasts despite yearly revenue decline and flailing car business</p><p>In the clearest sign yet that Tesla is pivoting away from its electric car business, CEO Elon Musk announced on Wednesday’s investor call that the company would discontinue production of its Model X SUV and Model S full-size sedan.</p><p>“It’s time to basically bring the Model S and X programs to an end,” Musk said. “We expect to wind down S and X production next quarter.”</p> <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jan/28/tesla-q4-earnings-estimates-elon-musk">Continue reading...</a></description>
